_instructions: Edit this file to update the scoring rubric in the EmployIQ app. After editing, copy the contents into the RUBRIC constant inside employability-index.html (clearly marked with === RUBRIC CONFIG ===). All scores are 0-100. Risk levels: LOW = high employability (70-100), MEDIUM = moderate (40-69), HIGH = low employability (0-39). weightsStandard is used for A/B/C/D (7 dimensions, must sum to 1.0). weightsCompany is used for E (9 dimensions, must sum to 1.0).

rubric.description: Risk thresholds derived from the EI-Index Risk Matrix. Each dimension maps user inputs to a risk level (LOW/MEDIUM/HIGH) and a base employability score. v1.2 adds futureCosts (all classifications), payroll and absence (Company-E only).

dimensions._coreNote: Core 6 dimensions — apply to ALL classifications (A/B/C/D/E)

lt100k.note: Low investment — high broad employability; UG-level roles accessible

lt300k.note: Medium investment — high employability for UGs; moderate specialisation required

gt500k.note: High investment — concentrated employability for Management graduates only

lt15.note: Low returns (1x-3x) — operational projects, high UG-level employability

gt15.note: Mid returns (3x-5x) — financial projects, UG+management level needed

r1530.note: High returns (5x-7x) — service/market projects, Masters and senior management only

t510.note: Long tenure — very stable, strong institutional knowledge, low disruption risk

t5.note: Established tenure — stable employment, low risk

t3.note: Mid-stage tenure — moderate risk, organisation still evolving

t1.note: Early tenure (1-3 years) — high disruption risk, limited role stability

ai.label: AI & Automation
ai.rubricSource: AI AND AUTOMATION row — Risk Matrix
ai.note: Highest-weight dimension — AI exposure is the primary driver of employability risk

low.note: Low AI exposure — higher employability across all role types

medium.note: Medium AI exposure — medium employability, proactive reskilling recommended

high.note: High AI exposure — low employability, significant automation displacement likely
> $100,000 operational cost — low employability, lean structures required

market.label: Market Potential
market.note: Larger markets concentrate talent at senior levels — broad workforce access decreases

m2.note: $2M market, ~10 resources — broad workforce access, many roles available

m25.note: $5M market, ~5 resources + high-performance teams — selective hiring

m510.note: $10M market, ~1 senior resource — concentrated senior talent, high risk for broad workforce

dimensions._newNote: New v1.2 dimensions — futureCosts applies to ALL classifications; payroll and absence apply to Company (E) only

futureCosts.label: Future Costs vs Work Potential
futureCosts.rubricSource: FUTURE COSTS VS WORK POTENTIAL row — Risk Matrix
futureCosts.note: Evaluates whether projected future costs are justified by the workforce's output potential

low.note: Low future costs — sustainable workforce potential

high.note: High future costs exceed work potential

payroll.label: Employee Payroll ($)
payroll.rubricSource: EMPLOYEE MONTHLY PAYROLL row — Risk Matrix
payroll.note: Monthly payroll per employee. Higher payroll reduces workforce scalability.

absence.label: Absence / Leave (days/mo)
absence.rubricSource: EMPLOYEE ABSENCE/LEAVE PER MONTH row — Risk Matrix
absence.note: Average employee absence days per month. Higher absence signals workforce instability.

classModifiers._description: Per-classification score adjustments applied on top of base dimension scores. Positive = more resilient in that dimension. Negative = more exposed. All 9 dimensions must be present for all 5 types.

weightsStandard._description: Weights for A/B/C/D classifications — 7 dimensions. Must sum to 1.0.

weightsCompany._description: Weights for Company (E) classification — 9 dimensions. Must sum to 1.0.
